note: System-assumption meta-node (authority-model Tier F): the truths the EVALUATORS rely on. A wrong assumption silently flips a control's state, so we model assumptions as first-class, monitored nodes. kind 'verifiable' has an executable check (holds/VIOLATED); kind 'accepted' is a known limitation/heuristic surfaced so it is never forgotten (not auto-checkable). affects_controls links an assumption to the controls it could corrupt ('*' = all Form-D-driven controls ONLY; Form-C-driven controls are listed explicitly, never matched by '*').

assumptions.statement: EDGAR Form D structured XML is at primary_doc.xml (strip the xsl-prefix from submissions' primaryDocument).
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Form D parse fails or yields the rendered HTML -> every control's state is silently wrong.

assumptions.statement: Form D field localnames are stable: entityName, federalExemptionsExclusions/item, hasNonAccreditedInvestors, dateOfFirstSale/value, relatedPersonInfo.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A field rename empties an evaluator input -> control silently flips to open/wrong.

assumptions.statement: EDGAR Form C structured XML is at primary_doc.xml (strip the xslC_X01/ styling-dir prefix from submissions' primaryDocument); nameOfIssuer + commissionCik resolve.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Form C parse fails or yields the rendered HTML -> the driver of the WHOLE funding-portal framework silently breaks (every issuer-side control wrong).

assumptions.statement: Form C field localnames are stable: nameOfIssuer, companyName, commissionCik/commissionFileNumber/crdNumber, offeringAmount/maximumOfferingAmount, issueJurisdictionSecuritiesOffering, signaturePerson, deadlineDate.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A field rename empties an evaluator input -> cf_cap / cf_single_intermediary / cf_state_notice / the issuer-side bad-actor screen silently flip with no drift signal.

assumptions.statement: cf_form_c_filed verifies only that a Form C EXISTS on EDGAR; the 'filed before the offering commenced' timing and the continuous Form C-AR legs are not publicly verifiable.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Existence could be read as a clearance. Mitigated: the control stays open and the note says 'NOT a clearance' - this registers that limitation as a first-class monitored caveat (parity with asm-badactor-rolling-window).

assumptions.statement: Form D federalExemptionsExclusions encodes Rule 506(b) as the literal code '06b'.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: exemption_basis control mis-detects the offering type.

assumptions.statement: eCFR versioner /versions/title-17.json?part=NNN returns content_versions[] with identifier + amendment_date.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Codified-law drift detection silently stops working (every node reads unverifiable).

assumptions.statement: Federal Register API returns results[] with a cfr_references field ([{title, part}]).
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Release/order drift mapping silently breaks (binding-but-uncodified gap reopens).

assumptions.statement: SEC Administrative Proceedings RSS (admin.xml) returns <item><title> entries.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Bad-actor drift monitor silently returns no matches (looks clean when it is blind).

assumptions.statement: EDGAR funding-portal registration uses the CFPORTAL form family: CFPORTAL (initial), CFPORTAL/A (amendment), CFPORTAL-W (withdrawal). Registered = a CFPORTAL exists and the latest is not a -W.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The SEC-registration leg of portal_finra_member mis-reads (false REGISTERED/WITHDRAWN) -> the control's state is silently wrong.

assumptions.statement: FINRA's 'Funding Portals We Regulate' page lists each portal in a div.multicolumn-container with an EDGAR link carrying filenum=<n>&type=CFPORTAL; presence on the list == FINRA member.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The FINRA-membership leg silently finds nothing -> portal_finra_member wrongly escalates (fails safe) or, if the structure shifts subtly, mis-attributes an entry.

assumptions.statement: Form Funding Portal (CFPORTAL) discloses fund custody in structured fields: escrowArrangements wrapping investorFundsContacts/investorFundsContactName (+ address). A registered broker-dealer files X-17A-5 on EDGAR (qualified third party per 227.303(e)(1)).
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The money-transmission leg (funds_qualified_third_party) mis-reads the custodian or the BD status -> control state silently wrong.

assumptions.statement: Form Funding Portal discloses regulatory history in four negative-event subtrees (criminalDisclosure / regulatoryActionDisclosure / civilJudicialActionDisclosure / financialDisclosure); affirmative leaves there are self-disclosed events (FP Rule 200). The scan is prefix-agnostic so a renamed field is not silently missed.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: If the subtrees are renamed/dropped, the scan finds nothing -> a false 'all-negative' clearance on portal_prohibited_conduct.

assumptions.statement: SEC funding-portal file numbers normalize to FINRA's display form by stripping leading-zero padding per hyphen part (007-00042 -> 7-42); this is the join key between the two registers.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A normalization miss makes the FINRA lookup fail -> portal_finra_member escalates ('not on list') rather than falsely satisfying. Fails SAFE (escalate, never a false clearance).

assumptions.statement: Bad-actor matching is case-insensitive substring matching; false positives are EXPECTED and resolved by human disambiguation.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Over-flags (handled: every hit is escalate-to-counsel, never a conclusion) or misses name variants (under-flags).

assumptions.statement: The AP RSS is a rolling window, not the full history; a non-match is NOT a clearance (historical SALI check pending).
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A non-match could be read as a clearance. Mitigated: output says 'NOT a clearance'.

assumptions.statement: The integration control uses a +/-180-day EDGAR window as a PUBLIC-SIDE proxy; it is not the legal facts-and-circumstances integration test.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Window misses an integration concern outside 180 days. Mitigated: control never 'satisfied'; counsel owns integration.
